txt: 'Tis a poor craftsman who blames his tools!
'Tis art.
'Tis not an act, my liege -- saving, perchance, he hath deceived his very self. This man is a zealot.
'Tis possible thou couldst persuade the lieutenant 'twas all a part of thy 'unorthodox' method. But can thou persuade *thyself*?
'Tis true sire. He knows nothing.
'Tis true sire. This man simply did not know what he was getting caught up in.
'Tis true, the laedy hath tried to avoid falsehoods.
'Twas stolen by some *artist*, surely. Best to pin the blame on them. Everyone *knows* you can't trust artists.
*But please do still tell me!*
*Definitely* lie. The truth is not credible.
*Laedyfolk* do not know of such things -- ballistics and weapons of war. She is too young and frail.
*Sssurveys* are a good way to fish for personal information, especially in the name of public sssafety...
...or lie. Something he's probably not very good at.
...the pause is long and dramatic...
10:15. It's understated.
A blatant lie, sire. Yet he tells it with such conviction. We'd believe him -- if we didn't know better.
A bullet deftly dodged, ssssire.
A convincing performance, bravo!
A courageous cop-man such as thyself surely did not flee in fear from a mere mortal!
A guy who has antagonized the Union in a Union-run town? Maybe it's political?
A lazy applause fills the room. You feel your hands shake as awareness of your body returns to you.
A lie. They were probably pawned off for something ssssuspicious.
A lie?
A look of betrayal. The secret has been revealed!
A man like Easy Leo could have said anything. Do not be restrained, sire.
A touch of absurdity there, nice!
A very good call. You must leave now, sire.
A very good call.
A very, very skilled thespian would detect a touch of drama when he says he doesn't know why it was conducted...
A white lie.
Again -- not a lie as far as you can tell.
All the goofing around is to avoid lying. It's a technique.
Alright -- Take two!
An excellent opportunity presents itself, sire! You could win the trust of the arch-liar, pretend to play into his hand -- then, should you wish, bend his efforts towards your own!
An infant could see he's not telling the truth -- but he's too scared to admit more wrongdoing.
And most importantly, never forget to rage-quit if the dice don't go your way!
Anyone could know that, sire. By looking around and calling the desk. I don't believe a single word she says!
Are we witnessing a performance, perhaps? Play along.
Arrest the liar! Now.
As always -- lie.
As you look at the folder, Evrart covers it with his hand and pets it. Is he trying to hide that it's not a real RCM folder? It certainly doesn't have the RCM stamp on it.
Attaboy.
Be abstract here. A bit of drama.
Be careful not to look too long into her soon-to-be-famous face, lest it make you look like an indecisive suitor.
Believeth in thyself, my liege! 'Twas all a clever ruse.
Belying the act, he's actually telling the truth. Or at least 0.00001% of it. He has a *little* speed. He's just not giving it to you.
Better not to gloat, sire. 'Tis *arrogance* that gives the play away. Be subtle.
But it is not hidden, is it, sire? You picked up on it. You should examine the pile of roofing material again.
But of course, sire. Looks like they're waiting for you to speak.
But promisssscuity leads to dddrama!
But we *are* awake, sire! She has been forthcoming -- with sordid details women usually conceal! Most *shocking* details of the sexual kind! We are a bulwark, un-breached...
By god, she's playing him too! Like a fiddle, sire... Who would have known?
C'mon. She obviously could. She already went into detail.
Can't write a scene without knowing the actors. Ask more, get comfortable around her.
Can't you see the *sire* is tired of these... fabrications. *RAC* is clearly what it says on the case files. These are official documents. There is no arguing with *official* documents.
Challenge him on this. Cause a scene.
Could it be all my fellows have failed? Alack and alas, sire! Forgive me! I shall do my best to end this pantomime...
Curious, my liege. Why did Cuno feed you this information?
Definitely, sire.
Degenerates? This man respects authority too much to see the truth inscribed upon thine own visage. Pretend thou art a paragon of virtue.
Did you hear that? The most pathetic applause in the world. Made of pity. No matter the time of day, or how small the audience -- this is a *pitiful* applause.
Do not act surprised. You know of these things, sire.
Do not let their squeals disturb your serenity. These are but simple peasants, sire.
Do you hear that? It's the most pathetic applause in the world, Harry. Made of pity. No one *liked* you.
Do you hear that? The most pathetic applause in the world. Made of pity. No one *liked* you -- they think you're a joke.
Don't be so sure...
Don't forget heated arguments escalating to physical confrontation with your friends.
Don't just jump to the folder. That's not smart, shows you're on the edge. Do some probing first.
Don't let them see you *flustered*, ssssire. Play along...
Don't let your imagination run wild here, sire. Keep it on track.
Don't tell him about the phasmid. He *will not* believe it. IT. DIDN'T. HAPPEN.
Done and done. No actual *communiques* will be sent, of course. That would be too dramatic.
Even before you can get the words out, everything gets scrambled in your brain.
Even with the intensity he's putting in those words, and the fidgeting in his pocket, the kid is unconvincing.

Every once in a while, it is like you can see glimpses of another guy under the guise of this *fighter for jobs*. He seems a more brutal, cunning and suspicious person. Just a hunch. Or... you just might be paranoid!
Everybody has something to hide. He *certainly* does.
Everyone *knows* you can't trust artists. They're nearly as bad as *clowns*.
Everything checks out here. It's all A-Okay. Good answer.
Everything checks out, sire!
Evrart's large hands are covering the folder, but the look on his face says *I know everything about you, Harry*.
Face it, sire, your little subterfuge isn't leading anywhere.
Fast and clean! A good one. Can't quite say it's a lie, but feels like one. She's better at lying than she'd like everyone to know.
For emphasis. A *ghost* is saying this. A shit-eating psychopathic ghost with an ace up his sleeve.
For god's sake. Lie.
For some reason, sire, he pays no heed to your objection. Continuing instead as if it *were* your ex wife. Strange...
For whatever reason she's lying to you about the fridge. Seems like you have to find your own way to the cellar.
Go long, go deep, go unconventional!
Gone forever... Sire, thee shall never findeth another mistress quaint like her!
Good, good. Of course. To outright declare yourself something does seem a bit too... *interesting* now, doesn't it? I won't trouble you any further.
Good, now take it a bit further. Take it home.
Good. Talk to him, demonstratively pick it up, then push it along with the other accusations. This is a good idea.
Gosh, she's so young, isn't she? She would look spectacular in a *starring role*!
He *did* mean Cindy the SKULL.
He *lost* it, he just doesn't know where it is, forget it, push on...
He *was* testing you. And you succeeded.
He appears sincere enough, sire. You could of course be wrong.
He believes the Hardie boys could've done it.
He can't know that. Maybe you're just younger on the photo.
He cannot believe it.
He chooses to believe what's best for her.
He considered it, but his priority was to get rid of the gun as quickly as possible -- albeit for a price.
He did.
He didn't *kill* him or anything, but there's something going on here.
He didn't do it. It's the truth.
He does not know *killer* related shit, sire. It's a falseness. He *may* know mysterious shit, however... it's hard to tell.
He doesn't care one way or another. For him, it's all one entertaining chamber play.
He doesn't know anything.
He doesn't really think you're a superstar or that you're well-off. He's only playing along because it's expedient.
He doesn't want your frail mind caught up in something here. Something *unconnected* to the case. But connected to this woman tuning out like that.
He doesn't.
He got the name from the Census Bureau and everything else from your behaviour here in Martinaise.
He had nothing to do with it.
He hasn't been particularly forthcoming before. He may well still be hiding something. After he's left it's too late.
He heard you. He just wants to hear you say it again. This is dramatic flare on his part... Right choice, we're in! Do it, sire!
He holds out his hands and blossoms his fingers, like a drama teacher setting the scene.
He is definitely not on La Puta Madre's take.
He is not enthused about the idea.
He is trying to avoid lying to you outright in case you really have been to his apartment.
He knows exactly what's going on.
He may be lying, but he's good at it. No twitching, no rushing, no uncalled-for details.
He may have some idea -- but he's not gonna get into it with you.
He might be wearing a disguise.
He really doesn't know.
He really doesn't.
He really has no idea who this Ruby is, sire.
He sincerely has no idea what you were talking about -- and he doesn't care either.
He speaks the truth, my liege.
He speaks the truth.
He thinks it's closer to 60-40. 60 they didn't do it.
He thinks you're stupid, sire.
He tries to keep his voice casual and businesslike, but it trembles ever so slightly.
He truly does not believe you are. Perhaps he shouldn't be so trusting...
He understands you're *concealing* something, sire.
He wants to see this tale through as much as *you*. Otherwise he'd have stopped this already. But he *cannot* let it drag out after this.
He was *wondering* about something business-related. About how much money he could make off one.
He was testing you. You succeeded.
He'll get it. Don't worry, just continue -- he's *gonna* be impressed.
He's an actor declaiming a soliloquy. See how you hang on his every word?
He's assuming a role to mock you -- or to entertain himself with your ignorance. Don't fall for it!
He's doing a good job here. A commendable performance of *I don't give a shit*.
He's faking.
He's gloating at you. Oh, it's so easy for Mr. Teacher!
He's hiding something.
He's just discovered that he can't think of any examples, though.
He's like an actor looking to the souffleur for his line.
He's looking for sssigns of deceit...
He's lying. But whatever, you don't really *need* his lame tracks.
He's lying.
He's lying... But there's no way to call him out on it.
He's meant everything he said before; but right now he not only means it -- he is sincere.
He's not 100% sure you're not indebted to this very, very bad person.
He's not comfortable with the possibility that you'll claim the find -- but he's lying about this even to himself.
He's not lying -- about not doing it himself.
He's not lying. He liked the end.
He's not. It's all but a ruse, sire. The lieutenant's peculiar sense of humor.
He's playing it cool, but he knows his ploy didn't work.
He's really shaking and everything!
He's really softening it up there.
He's setting it up for you...
He's surprisingly good at this. Not bad at all... Look at him lurching.
He's telling the truth. He hasn't seen anyone.
He's telling the truth.
He's trying to dive deep into the mysteries of his *shirt*!
He's trying to figure out if you're lying.
He's trying to hide the fact that he doesn't know.
He's working really, really hard to spin this, but it's not easy.
He's working really, really hard to spin this. It's not easy.
He's working really, really hard to spin this.
Her entire character has shifted. This young woman is cold as ice.
Her political overture has succeeded, we see?
Her voice is shaky. What is going on here?
Here comes a layup, sire! Fall not for it!
Here we go!
His eyes dart from left to right. This man isn't lying, but he *is* hiding something.
His remorse is insincere. That gent's relieved to beest able to moveth on my liege.
His smile, meant to convey total openness, is that of an experienced salesman.
Hmm... perhaps he is?
Hmph, her improv skills could do with some work. That was a *pathetic* performance. Are we supposed to be scared? NONE OF US ARE SCARED!
How about a tale of adventure, intrigue, and derring-do?
How about: "Gather around, kids!"
How could you expect anyone to stay around with all your *standard-issue* thoughts and opinions?
How dull! Entertainment isn't a wall to read. Where are the performers and pyrotechnics? No one will pay attention to this.
Hrmm, methinks my liege doth protest too much.
Hypothetically sssspeaking, you might add.
I don't know... you're a *pretty* good liar. Are you as good an *infiltrator* as you are a liar?
I hate to be the bearer of bad tidings, but I don't think she's perjuring herself.
I knew this would lead to something good!
I'm not picking up any theatre-craft here, sir. The pause is sincere.
I'm quite sure it wasn't like that, sire.
I'm wondering if the poetics come with the jacket or are they derived from something else entirely?
If it doesn't rhyme, you're not pronouncing it right.
If you have doubts, just give us *one* example of deceit! Just one, and we will gladly change our minds.
In the background you can hear the orchestra swell as the screen fills with the maiden's imploring eyes...
Interesting, sire. A bit like you then. An 'MC', for short.
Interesting, sire. It's as if he's lying -- to *protect* someone. He's not very good at it.
Is it... *wise* to share information about the case like this, sire?
Is this some kind of joke?
It feels great.
It just has to be said -- that was not a convincing line.
It was merely a *jest* -- horseplay! But now it looks like you're a total *psychopath*, jeopardizing the crime scene...
It was merely a *jest* -- horseplay! The impression of you being *a total psychopath* is wrong.
It was too real to be just a prank. Either we're dealing with a professional actress, or...
It would be *clever* of you to omit the boots altogether, sire. If you are to *keep* them for yourself -- as you ought to. You have deserved them more than anyone else!
It would take more subterfuge to trick the lieutenant.
It's a dry laugh. Mirthless. She's not even actually enjoying this. She only wants to hurt you.
It's all very organic -- her mannerisms, her movements. If she's acting, she's quite gifted.
It's because of the pale. He doesn't want your frail mind caught up in it -- and this woman has spent time *in* the pale. A lot of time.
It's easy to see he's telling the truth. He's kept his nose out of the dark stuff.
It's for show, my liege. He knows you need more than this to bust this guy. Later, perhaps?
It's hard to say if he doesn't believe you -- or doesn't care.
It's impossible to say if he's telling the truth, sire.
It's like he knows *nothing* about you. Stinks of a man doing what's best for him, not his clientele.
It's like he's lying to you, my liege. But he's slippery enough that there's nothing for you to grab hold of.
It's much more about the dark and deadly atmossssphere.
It's not gonna be as easy this time.
It's not her. She's not a Hardie girl. Definitely.
It's not insincere. He's actually giving it thought.
It's remarkable how he can keep a straight face. Take note -- the lieutenant is well versed in dead-panning. Many of the things he says could be meant in jest.
It's the voice of someone who has something to *hide* my liege.
It's true. We would have caught a lie. But... a *kind* heart is tricky.
Just keep it simple. He said they killed him. These uninitiated do not understand the fineries of Runic Science.
Keep on it, sire.
Klaasje only said they "maybe kissed". Someone is lying here.
LIE! LIE TO GET OUT OF IT!
Last words?
Let's call this one The Musician.
Lie! You have a witness.
Lie, sire. For no reason.
Lie. Just -- bide your time, ask something. Then lie.
Lies, lies, lies! He only speaks liessss.
Lies, sire! She cannot but love you. She  has said so a hundred times.
Loincloth? Now, you see, that's not really what a scab would say is it? Better not to press the issue further, sire. He seems... tense.
Look at her! Her hands, her nails, the cigarette... Every second you stall, you become more entangled in this spider's *web of lies*. Arrest her immediately!
Look at him raise his bushy brows. That's a lie. He's empty.
Lying over the phone? It's easy! Just say it like it's the truth -- and then it becomes it.
Make it... *lively*!
Maybe if she thought you're... *corrupt*?
Maybe it's just because she's so talented that she's been able to woo the curse?
Maybe twist the knife? Just in case. Make her more desperate.
Mazut is an antiquated term for heavy fuel oils -- this man has a barely suppressed performative streak. Or he just likes unusual words. Or both!
Me? What did I do? I'm merely a master thespian...
Me? What did I do?
My liege! You know what this case calls for? A para-detective!
My liege, it may be possible to *spin* this as some sort of, uhm... pedagogical exercise.
My liege, this woman has half-convinced herself already. All it would take is a little nudge...
No falsehoods are present. She's a novelty dicemaker and doesn't have anything to hide. Ask what you need.
No need to be defensive. The regularity, the brownness, the *cut-and-dry* have their appeal -- a very *standard* appeal.
No one would believe you without it.
No way. She's more. She can make decisions.
No, he really believes what he's saying -- he has completely accepted the narrative of transcendence through cocaine use.
No, no! He needs to say: "Five hundred Lears and I can't remember the first line!"
No, thou art Garte.
No, what *you* are is a *surprise*, get his wife on the phone...
No. That won't be necessary. Just leave her alone, she's been through enough. She doesn't need this... police brutality.
Not for a moment does he believe that's your real name. He casts it aside as an intradepartmental joke or a peculiarity he doesn't understand.
Not for one second did he believe there's an actual mega rich person somewhere in his containertown.
Not head, child, *headssss*!
Not in a million years does he believe Cuno is armed. Perhaps it's the best end he can hope for?
Not you at all, no-no-no!
Nothing changes in her tone as she says that -- as if it's just another piece of information to lay out for the world.
Now you're in a mess. The time hath not arrived. Sorry.
Now's your chance, ssssire. Which of your enemies would you most like to make trouble for?
Odd, he didn't buy it.
Odd... He doesn't seem to be lying, but there's something off here.
Oddly, it seems to be true.
Of course you do. And maybe some black shoes or a pristine white shirt to go with that. It's official: My lord's *copotype* is Regular Cop. I'll let everyone know. I'll send out a telefax.
Of course you do. Let's get right to it. My lord's *copotype* is Regular Cop. I'll let everyone know. I'll send out a telefax.
Of course you do. Maybe some *regulations* and a *usual* pistol to go with them? It's official: My lord's *copotype* is Regular Cop. I'll let everyone know. I'll send out a telefax.
Of course, it's your call. But he would have been *really* impressed.
Of course, sire, of course.
Of course, sire. Better not anger the Cuno with this silliness. We regret bringing it up. A thousand years of misfortune upon us for bringing it up!
Of course. Brinkmanship takes showmanship. We should have seen it before, in his office. He might well have been performing for us.
Of course. Politics is showmanship. He is constantly performing for us... and lying too. I am sorry I don't catch more of his falsehoods, there are just so *many*...
Oh god, do something quick, they're gonna start *that* again!
Oh my god, The Art -- they're using it against you. This is all a twisted phantasie!
Oh, those again? I have been trying to wean you off them.
Okay, he's too busy doing something else now. I meant Rhetoric. Anyway, don't listen to that guy -- we're right, she's saintly. Carry on.
Oooh, hidden things -- secrets, lies!
Or did you learn it in school? More importantly: who can prove if you did? Go with it -- you *deserve* some acclaim.
Patience. After the autopsy, before the body is taken away. There will be a *window* of opportunity, after the lieutenant has gone to sleep. I hope this has helped you, my liege.
People as oleaginous as Evrart seem like they're lying even when they're really being truthful.
Performing? Yesss, birdie!
Pff... It's surely an act. Ask questions. Tough ones, preferably.
Pretty okay, as far as lies go.
Pretty sure you could dial up the melodrama to max and he still wouldn't figure it out.
Psst! You forgot the subterfuge. You're saying this out loud.
Psst. Show him your stolen card. Now hast arrived the perfect moment!
Put on your prettiest face for the masses!
Rehearsal helps fine tune a performance. Learn your lines so they can flow out of you like Herc Zeitung's legendary soliloquy in 'Hoppla, jetzt komm ich!'!
SHUT UP! He's making a decision and it's *his* to make.
Safe and sound, sire. Surely you have not misplaced the witness.
Say: "Five hundred Lears and I can't remember the first line!!!"
Seditious talk! That was not even Our Wise Council, it was this shithead! This one. The next one.
See? You're so *regular* and vinyl-brown, he doesn't even want to talk to you about it.
See?!
Self-deceit, sire.
She acts chipper, but something's changed in her tone. A hidden worry.
She actually *did*!
She definitely called the cops. That was *task complete*. It could not hath been a *lie*. That is impossible.
She doesn't believe you.
She doesn't seem to be lying -- she's genuinely spooked by their disappearance.
She doesn't want to talk about this, but not because she has something to hide -- she doesn't want the guilt.
She feels interrogated now. It's hard to say if she's lying.
She hasn't. She hasn't gone up against three armed people before. And she believed you.
She hast not seen her, sire. 'Tis true.
She is trying to muster enthusiasm.
She iseth the fairest of them all. The ugliness of lies wouldst never escape her lips!
She isth a laedy most fair and juste!
She lied to you... about that *too*.
She looks a bit like a little girl who's just been complimented on her bike repair skills.
She might have been a tad disingenuous when she avoided talking about the bullet in his head *before...*
She probably didn't give you her real name either -- why would she? Arrest her immediately, before she further entangles you in her *web of lies*!
She probably didn't make the god damn call either. And her name is not Klaasje Amandou -- why would she give her real name? Arrest her immediately!
She really believes that.
She recites it like it's a poem, or like she's playing a role she's grown tired of.
She regresses and pretends she's a mythological creature when she's under too much stress.
She seems to be telling the truth, sire.
She so badly wants this to be over, she would have believed anything.
She sounds almost mocking when she says that.
She speaketh truth.
She thinks you're the gremlin child! What would he say to this?
She's being truthful. She is pressing them as hard as she can.
She's gearing up for a last stand. This is it.
She's genuinely rooting for you, sire!
She's heard of the murder, but did not see it, sire.
She's honest.
Simpler? No. This is another loose thread you will *never* connect!
Sire, he doesn't have to be wearing them *right now*.
Sire, he's telling the truth.
Sire, it just *went off*!
Sire, it would be an event most dramatic if you were to produce the bullet and dangle it before their very eyes!
Sire, may we suggest something? Think of the yelling-into-the-furnace as a *grand performance*. Use your chest voice, not your head voice. Sing from the diaphragm.
Sire, she doesn't believe things will ever improve around here.
Sire, she doth not believe it wasn't you.
Sire, the boy believes this to be true. He's giving you a last chance.
Sire, the boy has *not* really been in thine room. 'Tis but a falsification.
Sire, the tale is true.
Sire, the tent -- 'twas a *securitie risk*! And in here? Sanctuary! 'Twas only noble of you.
Slither up to her, you silver-tongued fiend. Show her what world-class perfidy looks like!
Smugly, sire. Raise it smugly.
So that's what it was, before! Him hiding something. He tries to make it look like he's a big deal in the Union and now the illusion is disintegrating before your -- and René's -- eyes.
So that's what that emotion was, before. It was *wishful thinking*.
So you say! But give us one example of deceit -- just *one* example.
Some manner of self-deceit is present in his thinking. Sounds like this Guillaume abandoned him and he doesn't want to admit it.
Someone dims the lights as the music starts. A lump's in your throat suddenly...
Someone dims the lights as the music starts... Okay. Here we go...
Something about the way she says it makes you want to sing.
Sort of -- but *he* doesn't have to know that!
Spit flies from your mouth to your moustache, your chest, the ground before you... No sound though.
Sssssire, you could learn much from thisss Mullen, if you underssstood who he issss....
Sssssssshe's lying. Probably hated it. They all did.
Still, seems suspicious... He may have it in for that guy. Or you may be paranoid. That is also a possibility, sire.
Stonewalling. Good technique.
Stop being such a fussy prude! You can't convince her without liessssss.
Stop choking or everyone will see you're a fake! All this *thespianism* isn't doing you any good!
Stop staring and say something dramatic and professional!
Stop staring at her eyes and say something pertinent to the case! This is making you look like an *amateur*.
Strange, he didn't buy it.
Strange. It's as if people don't believe a cop could be a socialist revolutionary.
Surely it was nothing, sire. Just paranoia.
Tell her how *ghasssstly* it was! You know it's what she wantssss to hear...
Test the waters here. Say that there's nothing to be worried about.
That note of contempt in his voice is sincere, sssire.
That's a lie.
That's a strange thing to suggest. The laedy iseth faere and juste!
That's all he knows -- that's all she's told him.
That's all she has to say on the subject. She *has* been thorough -- and truthful -- as far as we can see.
That's all she has to say on the subject. She's been thorough and truthful as far as we can see.
That's not the answer she was hoping for. Thought that you were some kind of patriot, or communard, did she?
That's right. Stern and merciless now, STERN AND MERCILESS as we reel her in.
That's the truth.
The *revolutionary* must have added a little lustre to it, for a ten-year-old Kitsuragi. He will never admit it though.
The Golden Mouth Man is a liar. He can't be trusted, sire. He only wants to humiliate you.
The air is thick with anticipation.
The city's too large for him to have left. But he wasn't *here* either... So where was he?
The glance is tender, yes, but tempered by something else, a thought she can't express even to him... Interesting.
The human lie-detector.
The kid is admitting falsehood, sire. He just doesn't know.
The lieutenant adopts a rodentine quality. Be cool, sire. He's getting *into* this.
The lieutenant issss *not* persuaded, sssssire.
The lieutenant issss not persuaded, sssssire.
The machinery of his mind is at work, sire! With swiftness the gears do turn, for to produce *deceit*! He seeks to hide his fondness for his favourite channel, Speedfreaks FM!
The man speaks in the artificial cadence of a professor -- or someone who's been on too many radio shows.
The nearly psychopathic way they can slip in and out of the act implies you're not the first victim.
The political gambit has failed.
The pose! The power! Yessssss, drag this part out and reel in your audience, darling. See if they will crack under the suspense.
The pre-sneeze hype is unreal.
The prints were his, you can see it in those eyes, he can't keep them from flickering, looking for something...
The reaction appears to be sincere, but... it's impossible to tell with this guy, honestly.
The sigh is tender, yes, but tempered by something else, a thought she can't express... Interesting.
The tape clicks into the carousel and then the music starts... God, a lump's in your throat suddenly...
The tape clicks into the carousel and then the music starts...
The tracks were recent -- but *not* worn down in the right foot like Ruby's. It's best to *omit* this pesky little polemic for now. Too *confusing*.
The truth, sire.
